channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Employment tribunal delays leave unfair dismissal cases pending for up to five years
item.description: In England and Wales, the Employment Tribunal service is experiencing unprecedented delays, leaving claimants waiting up to five years for a hearing. One affected individual is Catriona Ball, who file...

item.title: Yusuf Buhari Emerges APC Candidate for Sandamu/Daura/Mai’adua House of Reps Seat
item.description: Na so e be, Mr Yusuf Buhari, pikin wey be son of late former President Muhammadu Buhari, don officially carry APC ticket to contest Sandamu/Daura/Mai’adua Federal Constituency House of Representatives...

item.title: 1930s Street Fashion: Elegance and Style During the Great Depression
item.description: Street fashion in the 1930s represented a shift toward elegance and sophistication, moving away from the playful flapper silhouettes of the 1920s. Despite the economic challenges of the Great Depressi...

item.title: How Bees Inspire Smarter, Low-Power AI and Robotics
item.description: Even advanced AI can struggle when faced with unpredictable real-world conditions. A recent incident in San Antonio, Texas, saw a Waymo robotaxi drive into a flooded lane, highlighting that intelligen...

item.title: FG Launches AfCFTA Startup Acceleration Programme to Boost Nigerian Businesses Across Africa
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria don yan say the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) don waka past the negotiation stage and don enter serious implementation phase. Minister of Industry, Tra...

item.title: Florida International University bans GOP students over racist and violent group chat messages
item.description: Florida International University has banned two student members of the College Republicans after their involvement in a scandal involving racist and violent group chat messages. Abel Carvajal, a third...

item.title: UN General Assembly Votes to Support World Court on Climate Responsibility
item.description: On May 20, 2026, the United Nations General Assembly overwhelmingly voted 141-8 in favor of a resolution that supports an advisory opinion from the International Court of Justice (ICJ) stating that na...

item.title: CHADEMA Challenges Registrar Over Alleged Political Speech Violations
item.description: Dar es Salaam – CHADEMA, Tanzania’s main opposition party, has formally responded to the Office of the Registrar of Political Parties, strongly rejecting all allegations that it incited violence or br...

item.title: Senate sets schedule for VP Sara Duterte impeachment trial starting July 6
item.description: Senator Erwin Tulfo announced on May 21, 2026, that the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte is scheduled to begin on July 6 following a caucus among Senate members. Senate President Alan ...

item.title: APC hails smooth and competitive conduct of party primaries ahead of 2027 elections
item.description: After the nationwide APC primaries, National Chairman Professor Nentawe Yilwatda yan say di contests wey dey happen na di most competitive wey Nigeria don ever see. Di primaries cover all parts of di ...

item.title: David Seaman suggests a statue for Arteta if Arsenal win Champions League; Villa triumph in Europa League
item.description: A thrilling week in football has seen Aston Villa secure their first trophy in three decades with a commanding 3-0 victory over Freiburg in the Europa League final held in Istanbul. The match was atte...

item.title: Khalid Maqbool joins Economic Coordination Committee as member
item.description: On Thursday, the Prime Minister's Office confirmed that Dr Khalid Maqbool Siddiqui, the MQM-P leader and Education Minister, has been included as a member of the Economic Coordination Committee (ECC)....

item.title: Montreal Victoire wins first PWHL Walter Cup with 4-0 victory over Ottawa Charge
item.description: The Montreal Victoire captured their first Professional Women's Hockey League (PWHL) Walter Cup championship on Wednesday, defeating the Ottawa Charge 4-0 in Game 4 of the best-of-five series. Abby Ro...

item.title: The Importance of Youth Participation in South Africa’s Democracy
item.description: In South Africa, there is growing concern about the disconnection of young people from politics. While older generations continue to queue at voting stations, remembering the sacrifices made for freed...

item.title: 19th-Century Photographs Documenting the Sámi People by Roche and Bonaparte
item.description: In 1884, Prince Roland Bonaparte organized an anthropological expedition to Northern Norway (Finnmark) and Sweden to document the Sámi people. Most of the photographs were taken by G. Roche, the exped...

item.title: India Raises Concerns About Pakistan's Conduct at UN Security Council
item.description: At the Annual UN Security Council Open Debate on the 'Protection of Civilians in Armed Conflict', India strongly criticised Pakistan, highlighting its history of violence against civilians. Harish Par...

item.title: Remembering Jane Birkin: Icon of 1960s–70s Fashion and Film
item.description: Jane Birkin (1946–2023) was an emblematic figure whose unique blend of British cool and French elegance defined the style of the 1960s and 1970s. Born in London and later embraced by Paris, she gained...

item.title: Pakistan Condemns India at UN Security Council Over Terrorism, Occupation and Minority Rights
item.description: During the recent United Nations Security Council debate on civilian protection, Pakistan strongly countered claims made by India regarding Pakistan’s conduct. India’s Permanent Representative alleged...

item.title: Trump Administration Establishes Fund for Allies Following IRS Lawsuit Settlement, Raising Legal and Ethical Questions
item.description: On May 20, 2026, the U.S. Department of Justice announced the creation of a $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund,' intended to compensate individuals who claim they were targeted for political pros...

item.title: Melbourne bars targeted with $10,000 monthly extortion amid ongoing attacks
item.description: Melbourne’s nightlife scene has been under a spate of violent attacks, with bar owners now facing extortion demands of $10,000 per month. Messages received by the businesses warn that payment will rem...
